subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Exceptionally clear organization with explicit introduction, body and conclusion
Headings, sub-headings, numbered figures and appendix allow effortless navigation
Coherent logical development guides the reader through the investigation
weaknesses: Occasional digressions (e.g. personal anecdotes) slightly interrupt flow
Some paragraphs are overly long and could be split for even greater conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although there are minor digressions and a few dense paragraphs, the exploration is exceptionally well structured. The clear signposting of sections, consistent ordering of content and effective use of headings and figures lift it into the top band for Presentation.

strengths: Consistent and accurate use of mathematical notation (v(t), RMSE, r) throughout
Effective integration of equations, tables, graphs and diagrams into the narrative
Logical progression of mathematical argument with clear explanations
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies in unit formatting (km hâ»Â¹ vs km h<sup>â1</sup>)
A few axes labels lack units or use small fonts, reducing clarity slightly
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs appropriate symbols and calculus notation consistently, integrates multiple representations effectively and communicates the mathematics with clear logical structure. These qualities satisfy the criteria for the highest band in Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Designing and executing a personal driving experiment shows significant independent thinking
Manual fitting of a cubic model demonstrates initiative and creativity
Investigation clearly motivated by the studentâs own context and goals
weaknesses: Approach remains largely within conventional SL frameworks
Further innovation or extension beyond the chosen experiment would deepen personal engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration exhibits outstanding evidence of independent thinking and a highly personal approach. While the mathematics is standard SL content, the studentâs creative choices and initiative justify a top-band score for Personal Engagement.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on sources of error and pattern interpretation throughout
Thoughtful analysis of outliers and their impact on correlation
Clear evaluation of outcomes and practical implications with suggestions for improvement
weaknesses: Depth of some reflections is uneven across sections
Future implications could be explored more comprehensively
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful, well-reasoned reflection on results, limitations and implications throughout the exploration. The thoughtful analysis and clear evaluation meet the descriptors for the top reflection band.

strengths: Fully commensurate SL techniques (differentiation, regression, correlation) applied correctly
Thorough understanding evident in accurate derivations and step-by-step workings
Good justification of rounding, error bands and link between slope and fuel consumption
weaknesses: Minor conceptual slips in unit notation and occasional untested linearity assumptions
Could extend to alternative models (e.g., quadratic) for deeper mathematical insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student applies relevant SL-level mathematics effectively and accurately, demonstrating a thorough understanding. Small errors prevent the highest band, but the depth and correctness of techniques clearly warrant a band 4 for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations of regression, differentiation and correlation follow a logical sequence and are generally clear. Occasional verbose sentences and a few unit slips reduce absolute clarity, but the overall logical structure of the mathematical argument is good.

Derivatives are calculated accurately, regression equations are sensibly rounded, and r-value interpretation is correct. Some inconsistencies in units and the RMSE/negative-slope issue indicate small errors, so âgood understanding and mostly correctâ is appropriate.

The student presents equations, tables, graphs and a diagram of the incline. These representations are integrated into the discussion and referred to correctly, fulfilling âeffective useâ. Some axes lack units or have small fonts, preventing a âhighly effectiveâ judgment.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction, data collection, mathematical processing, discussion and conclusion. Transitions between sections are smooth and each paragraph advances the central aim. Occasional digressions (personal anecdotes, long explanations of LoggerPro) interrupt the flow, so the development is solid rather than exemplary.

Selecting unfamiliar statistical tools, learning LoggerPro and manually refining a model show creativity and initiative beyond basic requirements, but the mathematics itself remains standard SL content, so the descriptor âgood levelâ fits best.

Outliers are identified and the impact of removing them on r is examined. Limitations of curve-fitting and incline are discussed. Analysis is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ across the whole work; some arguments remain descriptive rather than critical.

The student evaluates the regression equation, derives a target acceleration and proposes improvements (using an accelerometer, flat road). Implications are noted but not fully explored, aligning with âclear evaluationâ rather than âcomprehensiveâ.

The student comments on patterns (e.g., plateau below 0.4 km h-1 s-1), sources of error and environmental implications, demonstrating meaningful reflection. Depth is uneven and does not sustain the âsubstantial critical reflectionâ required for the top band.

Appropriate symbols ( v(t), RMSE, r, L / 100 km ) and calculus notation are used throughout and units are usually shown. Minor inconsistencies in unit formatting (km h-1 vs km h<sup>-1</sup>) indicate that usage is âmostly consistentâ rather than flawless.

The investigation is clearly motivated by the studentâs own situation and decisions (choice of road, control variables, target acceleration) illustrate a personal approach. While evident, the approach is conventional rather than highly innovative.

Most material directly supports the stated aim and unnecessary repetition is limited. Nevertheless, lengthy narrative passages (e.g., detailed driving-test story, extended RMSE definition) reduce conciseness, so the piece is âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ rather than âhighly conciseâ.

Differentiation, polynomial regression, Pearson correlation, standard error bounds and trigonometric incline analysis are all relevant and applied appropriately to meet the aim. The range and depth exceed basic expectations but are not extensive enough for âcomprehensiveâ (5) or âexpertâ (6).

Designing and executing a personal driving experiment, manually creating a cubic fit when LoggerProâs fit was unsuitable, and setting a personal fuel-efficiency target exhibit significant independent thinking. The work does not reach the level of originality or depth required for âoutstandingâ.

Headings, sub-headings, numbered figures, tables and an appendix produce a very clear structure. The reader can locate data, working and conclusions effortlessly. Introduction, body and conclusion are explicitly sign-posted and consistently ordered, satisfying the descriptor of exceptional organisation.

Techniques used (calculus, statistical measures) are squarely at SL level and applied correctly in context. Minor conceptual slips (unit notation, assumption of linearity without testing alternatives) prevent a higher band but fulfill âfully commensurateâ.

annotations.comment: Rounding rationale based on measurement precision is well justified.
Unit notation remains inconsistent here; ensure uniform presentation of km h<sup>-1</sup> s<sup>-1</sup>.
Adding the regression line directly on the scatter plot would visually reinforce the model fit.
Formula for Pearson correlation is correctly presented; definitions of symbols are clear.
Using Â±2 SE bands to identify outliers is appropriate, though consider presenting formal confidence intervals.
Breaking the description of each trial into separate paragraphs would improve structure and reader engagement.
The description of least squares method is clear but would benefit from including the fitting equation.
Reflecting on learning curve for LoggerPro and manual fitting shows clear personal engagement.
Include units for the acceleration function to reinforce clarity in mathematical communication.
The title is descriptive but could be more concise. Consider shortening to improve focus and readability.
The image lacks a clear caption explaining its relevance. Adding a concise caption would enhance coherence.
The source citation for the image is formatted as a paragraph rather than a formal caption. Use consistent academic style for image references.
The anecdote about the instructor is engaging but interrupts the flow. Consider trimming or relocating to maintain relevance.
Clear and accurate explanation of L/100 km units enhances reader understanding.
The aim statement is split mid-sentence; revise so the complete aim appears on one page for coherence.
Thoughtful control of variables (road conditions, trials) shows significant independent thinking.
This paragraph is very long and dense. Break into smaller paragraphs to improve readability and organization.
How exactly was video data sampled at 1 s intervals? Clarify methodology to strengthen rigor.
The linear model $$e=ma+b$$ with definitions is clearly presented and well defined.
Unit notation appears as both âkm hâ»Â¹ sâ»Â¹â and âkm h<sup>-1</sup> s<sup>-1</sup>â. Standardize formatting.
Consider exploring a quadratic or root model if linear fit shows systematic residuals.
Explanation of LoggerPro usage is thorough but overly verbose. Trim narrative to focus on key steps.
Maintaining a consistent x-axis for comparing slopes is well justified and clearly explained.
Figure 1 effectively displays multiple trials but the caption is missing. Add descriptive caption.
Good qualitative link between slope of velocity-time graph and fuel consumption supports hypothesis.
The RMSE formula is correctly stated with clear notation and definitions.
The sentence ends abruptly at âdetrimental to theâ. Complete the thought to improve clarity.
Manually fitting a cubic function demonstrates initiative in addressing software limitations.
It would be helpful to include the explicit cubic equation used and its coefficients for transparency.
Power rule differentiation is applied accurately and shown step-by-step with correct simplification.
Discuss the practical significance threshold for r to justify grade of âstrong correlation.â
Derivation of regression coefficients m and b is mathematically correct and well formatted.
